title: Restore Product Truth and First-Run Integrity
architecture_decisions: villani.validation_coverage.v1 is the conservative command-to-requirement coverage contract; generic suite success alone does not prove unrelated behavior.
Focused probes are persisted, controller-scheduled, executed in the preserved candidate environment, and linked to candidate, requirement, evidence, and execution identity before final acceptance.
Probe behavior failures and probe infrastructure failures remain distinct and both fail closed; only behavior failures are candidate failures.
Known no-model deterministic verifier advisories are projected to non-accepting canonical actions without changing their raw value; malformed semantic-verifier output remains malformed.
villani.run_summary.v1 is the single projection consumed by CLI, web, reports, static viewers, support artifacts, and Flight Recorder.

Classification persists raw signals and effective classification after generic normalization of duplicate criteria, repository breadth, subsystem count, risk, dependency uncertainty, validation burden, and scope.

Local installation stages a complete environment and configuration, verifies mandatory imports and executables, then atomically activates or rolls back; runtime and development dependency profiles are separate.
The release gate installs only built artifacts into clean environments and uses deterministic local model fixtures without an onboarding acceptance bypass.

schema_and_configuration_migrations.compatibility: Legacy repository-validation evidence migrates conservatively with unknown coverage and never gains inferred requirement proof.
Older bundles remain readable through a synthesized projection; missing and unknown accounting stay explicit rather than becoming zero.
Optional summary and validation-coverage artifact references were added in normative and packaged schemas.
Existing working environments and configuration files are preserved until staged validation succeeds; rollback restores the prior state.

tests_added_or_extended: Exact installed-user onboarding contradiction regression, retained as a successful selected-delivery regression after the fix.
Validation-coverage schema, deterministic linkage, conservative unrelated-requirement behavior, and legacy migration tests.
Focused-probe scheduling, plugin-boundary forwarding, candidate/requirement/evidence linkage, redundant-probe suppression, and behavior-versus-infrastructure tests.
Cross-surface CLI, web, Markdown report, static artifact, and Flight Recorder summary reconciliation tests.
Easy, medium, and hard classification fixtures with raw/effective signal persistence.
Interrupted installer publication, missing mandatory dependency, rollback, runtime/development profile separation, and exact repair-command tests.
Clean installed-user gate, built-artifact-only assertions, screenshot capture, delivery, doctor, dead-letter, and evidence secret-scan tests.

skips: Villani Ops: 2 host-capability skips for unavailable Unix-style special-file/socket behavior on Windows.
Villani Code: 1 opt-in Claude Code smoke test skipped because RUN_CLAUDE_CODE_SMOKE was not enabled.

user_facing_before_after.before: A valid implementation-and-test patch with passing authoritative validation and semantic approval was rejected for unscheduled evidence; the terminal displayed 0 passed and 0 failed checks while the artifact contained a pass; the trivial task classified hard.
user_facing_before_after.after: The same generic workflow uses conservative coverage to prove linked changed behavior and tests, suppresses only redundant probes, selects one proved candidate, reports 1 passed and 0 failed checks consistently on every surface, and classifies easy absent a real repository risk signal. Unrelated or uncertain requirements still request evidence or fail closed.

risks.security: No secrets were logged or found in release evidence; repository, package, and onboarding evidence scans passed. Focused probe output remains bounded and redacted.
risks.privacy: Run bundles and support artifacts may contain repository paths and task content by design; credential values are withheld and redacted.
risks.data_loss: Installer publication and configuration activation are staged and rolled back; delivery in the release gate is non-destructive. No automatic materialization occurs for unproved candidates.
risks.compatibility: Legacy run bundles and configurations remain readable. Synthesized legacy coverage is conservative and may show unknown/not-run rather than retroactively proving behavior.
risks.residual: Coverage linkage intentionally prefers false negatives over false positives. Execution providers remain process-level isolation unless a stronger configured provider is selected.

validation_environment_cleanup.reason: The automatic approval reviewer rejected both cleanup operations after its approval-usage limit was reached.
validation_environment_cleanup.metadata_only_git_status_reason: Each worktree hash equals its index hash and git diff is empty; the read-only .git sandbox prevented an index-stat refresh.

assumptions: The repository's existing versioned v1 schema directory remains the correct location for additive PT0 contracts.
A changed test executed by an authoritative command is coverage only when the deterministic evidence graph links requirement, changed behavior, changed test, and command.
Unknown accounting remains null with an explicit accounting status and is never projected as numeric zero.
